title: Sales-as-the-big-domino — VC founder talk, Aug 15 2019
html:
22-minute recording of a female founder/VC speaking to a Portland-area entrepreneurship audience about how she went from negative bank account / unpaid team to $750K pipeline in one quarter by learning sales the hard way — flying to a SF accelerator, knocking on CEO doors, then 75%-of-time-on-sales discipline. Yahir captured this during the same week as the YBW leadership talk; both feed his teenage business-archetype scaffolding.

TL;DR

  • Speaker tells the founding story: payroll missed, team couldn't eat, she spent the night realizing it was 100% her responsibility — went to a SF sales accelerator on a linkedin cold-invite, learned the coffee knock on doors approach from successful CEOs.
  • Built a $750K pipeline in 3 months by spending 75% of her time on sales only and ignoring investor pageantry; the moment she emailed investors saying she was no longer raising, term sheets came in. Frame: "pretend no one will ever invest in you and just build."
  • "Someday is a disease that will take your dreams to the grave" — the speaker's signature line; sits next to memento mori decision filter and fear of not doing in Yahir's later concept set, and quotes Reid Hoffman's "if you're not embarrassed by your first version you launched too late" (lives next to learn to run before flying).
  • Closing challenge: "what's one thing you can do today?" — frames sales as the big domino that knocks over the website / packaging / investor problems downstream. This is the exact mental model Yahir is reaching for in the 2026 Elijah call when planning aeo agency tool distribution.

Key quote

"Someday is a disease that will take your dreams to the grave with you."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Yahir at 17 absorbing the sales > everything else gospel — directly traceable to his later aeo agency decision to prioritize tool-in-front-of-customers over fundraising.
  • The "sit on the bench eating popcorn" → "get on the court" metaphor recurs almost verbatim in Yahir's own later self-talk in the time capsule recording.
  • Pair with the YBW leadership talk — same week, same teen-Yahir audience, complementary lessons (commitment + sales).

Open questions

  • Who is the speaker? Recording captures "I now run a VC fund." Worth identifying — likely Portland-area angel/seed fund circa 2019.

fear of not doing · do things that dont scale · max effort commit · learn to run before flying · memento mori decision filter · aeo agency · sahil bloom
